channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation initiative after vessel attacked
item.description: Iran warns vessels to use only Tehran-approved routes as IMO halts Hormuz evacuation after ship struck.

item.title: Venezuela quake leaves La Guaira in almost total devastation
item.description: La Guaira, the region hardest hit by two earthquakes in Venezuela, has been left devastated.

item.title: Dutch fans party in Kansas City ahead of World Cup clash with Tunisia
item.description: Netherlands supporters got the party going in Kansas City, US ahead of their final group stage game against Tunisia.

item.title: Netanyahu: ‘We will remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza as long as required’
item.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israeli forces will maintain a presence in southern Lebanon, Syria and Gaza.

item.title: Ashura ceremonies intertwine faith and support for state in Iran
item.description: State-backed Ashura events showcase leaders killed by US, Israel, seen as martyrs in the mould of Imam Hussein.

item.title: Kenya’s Kandie gets seven-year ban for doping violations
item.description: Kibiwott Kandie initially faced an eight-year ban split evenly across two violations, but received a one-year reduction.

item.title: Has Marco Rubio’s tour of the Gulf reassured US allies?
item.description: The US secretary of state tells Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) states that the Iran deal will ensure their security.

item.title: ‘Truly terrifying’: Caracas confronts the aftermath of Venezuela earthquake
item.description: Devastated residents recall the moment when they experienced the twin earthquakes that toppled buildings and killed 188.

item.title: Kenya arrests more than 350 as people mark anniversary of deadly protests
item.description: Demonstrators mark the second anniversary of a 2024 protest where 60 people were killed by security forces.

item.title: Bedouins protest Israel’s home demolition policy in the Negev desert
item.description: Bedouins protest Israel’s home demolition policy in the Negev desert

item.title: Krieg: For Iran control of the Strait of Hormuz is about the spoils of war
item.description: Andreas Krieg said Iran possesses sufficient geographic and military leverage in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: ‘Concrete breaking off walls’: Survivors describe Venezuela’s earthquakes
item.description: The Venezuelan government has loosened restrictions on social media as residents search for missing loved ones.

item.title: Russia set to return to FIFA competition at inaugural U-15 World Cup
item.description: FIFA's inaugural U-15 World Cup in October is opened to all of its member associates, paving way for Russia's return.

item.title: US Supreme Court scales back Roundup cancer lawsuits in victory for company
item.description: More than 100,000 plaintiffs had filed cases in US state and federal courts alleging cancer link to weedkiller Roundup.

item.title: Ukrainian attack on Crimea kills five, Russian officials say
item.description: Attacks continue on the ground as Moscow expels Romania’s consul general in a tit-for-tat move.

item.title: Pragmatic choice: Israel’s war backfires as Gulf backs US-Iran deal
item.description: A war meant to isolate Iran instead deepened Gulf pragmatism and strengthened support for diplomacy.

item.title: How the HPV vaccine changes how we think about cancer
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Linh Nguyen explains how the HPV vaccine changes the way we think about cancer.

item.title: Venezuela struggles to respond to devastating twin earthquakes
item.description: Noris Soto says authorities appear to lack a clear response plan in the aftermath of two powerful earthquakes.

item.title: Rubio: Gulf countries don’t support Strait of Hormuz tolls
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said all Gulf countries oppose a toll in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israeli forces shoot and kill Palestinians in occupied West Bank, Gaza
item.description: The deaths come amid escalating Israeli military raids and settler attacks across the occupied territories.

item.title: US Supreme Court paves way for government to block asylum seekers at border
item.description: Liberal justices say the ruling 'circumvents' US law by allowing agents to prevent asylum seekers from filing a claim.

item.title: Escaping Darfur: Mothers face starvation under trees in Chad
item.description: Fleeing scorched earth, Darfur’s mothers share harrowing tales of starvation and survival in Chad.

item.title: Spain vs Uruguay at World Cup 2026: match prediction, team news and lineups
item.description: Past winners Uruguay and Spain face off in Guadalajara, battling for top-spot in Group H and a kinder knockout draw.

item.title: They fled war as child refugees, now they’re playing at World Cup 2026
item.description: In a World Cup for everyone, as it has been dubbed by FIFA, players carry historic stories of war and displacement.

item.title: Israel kills three in Lebanon as Rubio praises progress in Washington talks
item.description: Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reports three people killed after an Israeli attack on a car in Nabatieh.
